content: base64 -i example-config.txt > base64-encoded.txt more base64-encoded.txt Y29uZmlnIHRleHQK base64 -d -i base64-encoded.txt > base64-decoded.txt more base64-decoded.txt config text
summary: base64 -i example-config.txt > base64-encoded.txt more base64-encoded.txt Y29uZmlnIHRleHQK base64 -d -i base64-encoded.txt > base64-decoded.txt more base64-decoded.txt config text
title: Encode Decode With Base64

content:
This is a quick way of getting cdp or lldp information from a switch by plugging in your network cable in a port. Results may vary, depending on the configuration of the port.
windows example locate your interface with
C:\Program Files\Wireshark> .\tshark.exe -D start capturing:
PS C:\Program Files\Wireshark> .\tshark.exe -i "\Device\NPF_{***}" \ -Y "(lldp) or (cdp)" 711 15.099343 Cisco_12:31:03 → CDP/VTP/DTP/PAgP/UDLD CDP 502 \ Device ID:switch-01.domain.com Port ID: GigabitEthernet0/3
summary:
This is a quick way of getting cdp or lldp information from a switch by plugging in your network cable in a port. Results may vary, depending on the configuration of the port.

title: Tsharking Cdp and Lldp

content:
adding ssh key to githhub SSH Setup:
on your Ubuntu machine, generate a new SSH key pair by running the following command in your terminal:
ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "your_email@example.com" once generated, add your new key to the ssh-agent:
eval "$(ssh-agent -s)" ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_rsa copy your SSH public key:
cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub add key to github
clone the repository with the command:
git clone git@github.uio.no:YourUserName/YourRepoName.git
replace “YourUserName/YourRepoName.git” with your Github username and the repository name.
summary:
adding ssh key to githhub SSH Setup:
on your Ubuntu machine, generate a new SSH key pair by running the following command in your terminal:
ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "your_email@example.com" once generated, add your new key to the ssh-agent:
eval "$(ssh-agent -s)" ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_rsa copy your SSH public key:
cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub add key to github
clone the repository with the command:
git clone git@github.uio.no:YourUserName/YourRepoName.git
replace “YourUserName/YourRepoName.git” with your Github username and the repository name.
title: adding ssh keys to github

content: setting timezone in ubuntu timedatectl set-timezone your_time_zone
